config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but I do not think it should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse and unfairness. In my view, individual rights are the guardrails that stop majorities, governments, or communities from trampling people who are unpopular or vulnerable. A healthy society should protect both the common good and personal rights, not treat them as if one always cancels the other.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai secara umum, karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas utama yang tidak boleh begitu saja dikalahkan oleh klaim “kepentingan masyarakat.” Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi sejarah juga menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas diberi prioritas mutlak, hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, diskriminasi, dan pembungkaman kelompok kecil. Menurut saya, yang benar bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara otomatis, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang ketat: kepentingan umum boleh dibatasi hanya ketika memang perlu, proporsional, dan tetap menghormati hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or pressure people to conform. Of course, we do value harmony, family, and social order, but those values lose their moral force if they are built on trampling basic rights or ignoring minority voices. For me, the right position is balance: society can set limits when there is real harm, but individual rights should remain the default protection, not something the majority can override whenever it wants.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari perspektif Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan mayoritas yang berlebihan. Poin tentang harm dan keteraturan memang penting, tetapi saya tidak mau menerima logika bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” orang bisa begitu saja dibungkam atau dipaksa tunduk pada tekanan sosial. Bagi saya, hak individu adalah garis batas yang melindungi minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer, sementara kepentingan publik hanya sah bila benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merusak hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over the individual by default, because in Indonesia that kind of thinking is often used to pressure people into silence and obedience. I respect harmony and communal responsibility, but I have seen how “public interest” can become a vague excuse for censorship, social shaming, or ignoring minority rights. A society is stronger when it protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a clear, real harm that justifies it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif Amerika Serikat, saya curiga pada argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” karena terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan mayoritas membungkam minoritas, dan itu bukan perlindungan sosial melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Poin tentang harm memang valid, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan hak harus sempit, jelas, dan proporsional, bukan diberi cek kosong atas nama masyarakat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi hak dasar dulu, lalu menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
